brand.name: 40/400 Meals
brand.audience: busy moms and high-protein eaters who want simple meals that fit real life

recipe.title: Chocolate Protein Mug Cake
recipe.summary: A warm, chocolatey, high-protein mug cake made in the microwave. It tastes like dessert, fits the 40/400 method, and is ready in minutes.
why_youll_love_it: Easy enough for a busy weeknight

ingredients.notes: Use a protein powder you like the taste of.
Egg whites will usually lower calories slightly.
Helps keep the mug cake moist.
Add more if the batter is too thick.

instructions: Spray a microwave-safe mug with nonstick spray.
Add protein powder, oat flour, cocoa powder, and baking powder to the mug.
Stir in egg, Greek yogurt, and milk until smooth.
Microwave for 45 to 75 seconds, depending on your microwave.
Let it cool for 1 minute before eating. The center should be soft but not raw.
tips: Do not overcook. Protein mug cakes can get dry quickly.
If the batter looks too thick, add another tablespoon of milk.
Use a protein powder you already enjoy because the flavor comes through.
Let the mug cake rest for a minute before eating so the texture sets.

storage.notes: This recipe is best eaten fresh right after cooking.

busy_mom_test.notes: This is a strong quick dessert option, but it is not ideal for meal prep.
